description: Qinhuai River sightseeing experience, admire ancient architecture and night views along the banks, and feel the charm of the Six Dynasties ancient capital.
Showcase of Nanjing Yunjin intangible cultural heritage, with demonstrations of Yunjin weaving techniques.
Mausoleum of Zhu Yuanzhang, the founding emperor of the Ming Dynasty, a UNESCO World Heritage Site and a model of Ming and Qing imperial tombs in China.
Residence of Chiang Kai-shek and Soong Mei-ling, featuring French architectural style, known as the "First Villa of the Far East."
Double-helix scenic pedestrian bridge on the Yangtze River, a modern architectural landmark offering views of the river and city skyline.
Nanjing's famous food street, gathering various specialty snacks and night market delicacies, offering an authentic taste of Nanjing.
China's largest imperial garden lake, known as the "Pearl of Jinling," with diverse seasonal scenery.
Nanjing's most bustling commercial center, offering shopping, dining, and entertainment all in one place.
Nanjing's renowned snack street, featuring authentic Nanjing specialty foods, a recommended dining spot by locals.
Mausoleum of Dr. Sun Yat-sen, memorial site of the founding father of the Republic of China, and the core attraction of the Zhongshan Scenic Area.
Preserved street district of Ming and Qing ancient architecture, blending traditional residences with modern commerce, offering a taste of old Nanjing.
One of China's three major museums, rich in collections including the History Hall, Art Hall, Republic of China Hall, and six other exhibition halls.

attraction_name: Zhongshan Scenic Area Sun Yat-sen Mausoleum Music Terrace
description: Open-air music stage built during the Republic of China period, featuring exquisite architecture and a serene surrounding environment.
Specialized museum showcasing the history and culture of the Six Dynasties, with a strong modern design aesthetic.

attraction_name: Purple Mountain Observatory of the Chinese Academy of Sciences
description: China's birthplace of modern astronomy, offering panoramic views of Nanjing and serving as a science education base.
Sacred site commemorating China's New Democratic Revolution, a solemn place to honor revolutionary martyrs.
Ruins of an ancient Southern Dynasty temple, now the cemetery for fallen soldiers of the National Revolutionary Army, with the Linggu Pagoda as its iconic structure.
